run_metadata.notes: Threat model: this endpoint is internet-facing and authenticated by GitHub HMAC only. The function writes to Supabase using service-role credentials, so authenticated webhook calls can enqueue privileged work. Main risks are forged requests (already tracked as fixed_pending_verify in open_findings) and replay/duplicate processing of previously valid deliveries. A realistic adversary is anyone who can obtain a previously valid signed payload+header pair from logs, misconfigured observability, or internal tooling.

agent.name: security-and-privacy-auditor
agent.role: Identify practical security and privacy risks with evidence-backed findings and remediation.

findings.title: Missing webhook delivery replay protection allows duplicate authenticated queueing
findings.description: The handler validates HMAC but does not enforce idempotency or replay protection using `x-github-delivery` (or equivalent unique event key). A previously valid signed request can be resent multiple times and each replay inserts new `audit_runs` rows. Realistic attack scenario: a contractor or internal service with access to webhook logs copies one legitimate payload and signature, then replays it repeatedly to trigger duplicate queued audits and unnecessary spend without needing the webhook secret.

proof_hooks.summary: Request processing verifies signature and immediately enqueues runs; no dedupe key check or persistent replay guard is present.
Replay shape using one previously valid delivery
steps: Capture one legitimate webhook request body and its `x-hub-signature-256` header from authorized logs in your own environment.
Send the exact same POST request to the function endpoint multiple times.
Observe each replay returns success and inserts additional queued rows because no delivery-id uniqueness check is enforced.

repro_steps: Use a known-valid payload+signature pair from a prior legitimate webhook delivery in your own stack.
Verify duplicate `audit_runs` records are created for the same logical event.
findings.impact: Authenticated replay can inflate queue volume, consume compute budget, and create noisy duplicate audit activity.

suggested_fix.approach: Require `x-github-delivery` and enforce idempotency: store delivery IDs (or event GUIDs) with a unique constraint and reject already-seen IDs before enqueue. Optionally include `(repository.id, delivery_id)` composite uniqueness with TTL/retention.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Ensure dedupe write/check is atomic to avoid race-condition duplicates under concurrent delivery retries.
tests_needed: Integration test: same delivery ID posted twice only enqueues once.
Concurrency test: parallel duplicate deliveries still produce one enqueue.

history.notes: New finding from focused re-audit; prior HMAC bypass finding already exists and was not duplicated.

next_actions.action: Implement delivery-id idempotency with unique persistence before `audit_runs` insert.
next_actions.rationale: Prevents authenticated replay from creating duplicate queued audits.
